item.title: Union calls for urgent response after Ashley Youth Detention Centre staff hospitalised
item.description: A union claims management at Tasmania's youth prison did not follow its own short-staffing policy, despite an incident occurring only a day after workers took strike action due to safety concerns.

item.title: PFAS foam was used to extinguish a tanker crash just off the M1 Motorway at Ourimbah, on December 8, 2000.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Nine-year-old world record deadlift
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Painkillers and HRT therapies among drugs not being subsidised under PBS
item.description: Painkillers, HRT therapies and incontinence medicines are among the drugs that will not benefit from the new maximum $25 script price.

item.title: Openly gay men in elite sport are rare, leaving women to lead the way for queer inclusion
item.description: While high profile instances of homophobia in elite men's sport continue, leading female athletes are hoping to help create more inclusive spaces for all.

item.title: Cooper Woods' mum reacts after stunning gold medal win in Men’'s Moguls
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sophie Hyde’s dad came out when she was a baby. It shaped three generations.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: News quiz: Year of the Horse traditions, zodiac facts and festive customs across Asia
item.description: How much do you know about the Year of the Horse and unique Lunar New Year traditions in Asian countries? Put your knowledge to the test with these 10 questions and see if you're a true Lunar New Year expert!

item.title: Melbourne man sets up West Gate Bridge livestream from his driveway
item.description: A man in Melbourne's west has set up a camera from his driveway to broadcast live vision of traffic on the West Gate Bridge, attracting thousands of views within weeks.

item.title: Alcoa hit with record fine for unlawfully clearing WA jarrah forests for bauxite mine
item.description: US mining giant Alcoa is forced to pay $55 million after unlawfully clearing parts of a Western Australian native forest to mine bauxite.

item.title: Wayne Gardner disappointed Victoria won't host Australian MotoGP
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scientists hopeful of a sepsis drug breakthrough
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: Eternity, "Wuthering Heights" and new Meg Mac
item.description: From a cursed vagina to a classic retold, there's plenty of culture to dig into this weekend.

item.title: This week in history quiz: Do you remember these past events?
item.description: How well do you remember Trainspotting, the story of an iconic photo and the M*A*S*H finale?

item.title: Horse endoscopy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Could Canada and Australia form a critical minerals supergroup?
item.description: Canberra's decision to sign a bilateral agreement with Canada to join forces on everything to do with critical minerals could prove to be a forerunner of things to come.

item.title: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro speaks to ABC Sport's Amanda Shalala.
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: Could Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor be removed from the line of succession?
item.description: Is it possible to remove him? The short answer is yes — but it would most likely be a time-consuming process involving many parliaments passing legislation.

item.title: Yuin elder and public educator Aunty Ruth Simms publishes first children's book, Muttonfish Magic
item.description: Aunty Ruth Simms has published her first book, Muttonfish Magic, as she prepares to celebrate five decades in the public education system.

item.title: Farmer hopes to represent Australia snowboarding at the Paralympics four years after losing leg
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Born in a Taxi and Osadía to bring roving performance magic to WOMADelaide
item.description: Melbourne's Born in a Taxi will join Spain's Osadía in bringing their unique performances to the world music festival next weekend, but they point out street theatre has not always been received well.

item.title: What is the future for Tennis Australia?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Queensland Reds defeat Highlanders, Western Force beat Moana Pasifika in Super Rugby
item.description: The Queensland Reds and Western Force have recorded their first wins of the Super Rugby Pacific season in round three.

item.title: Tennis star Destanee Aiava 'scared' to walk on court because of death threats
item.description: Destanee Aiava was once called the best 14-year-old female tennis player in the world. Just over a decade later, she is walking away from the sport and calling out its toxic culture.

item.title: Australia's almond industry cracks $1.3 billion mark as Chinese demand rises
item.description: Australian almonds are breaking records as the trade war between the United States and China rolls on.

item.title: Women trafficked, kids taught to shoot in PNG's tribal warfare industry
item.description: The proliferation of illegal high-powered guns is fuelling tribal warfare across Papua New Guinea and warlords claim children as young as eight are being trained to carry the weapons.

item.title: Former NSW prison officers say system protects abusers and silences women
item.description: Current and former NSW prison officers describe what they say is a toxic workplace culture and a system that shields officers accused of serious misconduct, rather than holding them accountable. They argue it has driven younger officers out of the service.

item.title: Seventh narco sub discovered in the Pacific as Australia's cocaine habit drives heavy traffic
item.description: At least seven narco subs have been discovered in Pacific waters near Australia in the past 18 months, with experts warning that Australia's cocaine demand means more are on the way.

item.title: How one change to Disability Discrimination Act could make it less 'daunting'
item.description: Australia's Disability Discrimination Act is decades old, with reform "well overdue". Removing the burden of proof could be the first step.

item.title: Daniil Medvedev among tennis stars stranded in Dubai after air strikes, Zimbabwe cricketers stuck in India
item.description: Daniil Medvedev says he is unsure when he will be able to leave Dubai after the exchange of air strikes between the US, Israel and Iran shut down the UAE airport.

item.title: Queensland Reds enforcer Lukhan Salakaia-Loto shines to start Super Rugby Pacific season
item.description: Playing with a chip on his shoulder and a point to prove to national selectors, Lukhan Salakaia-Loto is not wasting any opportunities early in the Super Rugby Pacific season.

item.title: Can you ask a neighbour to remove or trim a large tree for safety or other concerns?
item.description: Whether you're worried about how old mate's tree next door will hold up in the summer storms, or it's just creating more work for you on the weekend, knowing where you stand can be helpful.

item.title: Jackie O announces departure from breakfast show she hosted with Kyle Sandilands
item.description: In an ASX announcement, ARN Media saysHenderson "has given notice she cannot continue to work with Mr Sandilands".

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Blood moon total lunar eclipse lights up Australian sky
item.description: Australians looking to the sky on Tuesday night saw the Moon turn a reddish-orange hue, in the last total lunar eclipse until 2029.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Vietnam typifies perseverance as it aims for new heights
item.description: Vietnam's women's football team has spent the past 20 years patiently building success out of the spotlight, and is ready to take centre stage at the 2026 Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Players flee court at UAE tennis event after security alert amid Middle East war
item.description: An ATP Challenger tournament in Fujairah is halted abruptly due to a security alert, forcing players and officials to run from the courts to designated safe areas.

item.title: Artificial intelligence detects high breast cancer risk in women given all-clear
item.description: A new Australian artificial intelligence tool could transform breast cancer screening by offering a more personalised and accurate way to identify risk.

item.title: Ex-Wallabies coach Dave Rennie to lead All Blacks through to 2027 Rugby World Cup
item.description: Three years after he was sacked as Wallabies coach, Dave Rennie is put in charge of the All Blacks for the next two seasons.

item.title: Big smiles for farmers as downpour soaks parched paddocks in Victoria
item.description: A big downpour across much of drought-affected Victoria puts smiles on farmers' faces.

item.title: Adelaide Fringe ticket sales 'drop off a cliff' because of fizzer rain forecast
item.description: A dire weather forecast for last weekend in Adelaide combine with ongoing cost of living pressures to deliver a huge drop-off in Adelaide Fringe ticket sales, organisers and artists say.

item.title: How airlines determine if it is safe to fly from conflict zones
item.description: A flight from Dubai to Australia has taken off, becoming the first commercial plane to carry Australians home since the Middle East conflict began.

item.title: Dennis Cometti speaks to the ABC in 1984
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Attorney-general pays taxpayer back $8,000 for family flights
item.description: Figures from the expenses watchdog confirm Attorney-General Michelle Rowland has repaid about $8,000 spent on flights for family travel from Perth to Sydney.

item.title: Mark Carney warns Australia and Canada face 'subordination' by global superpowers
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney calls for middle powers to use their sovereignty in a Sydney speech echoing his Davos intervention.

item.title: Emotional scenes as first commercial flight out of Middle East since war began lands in Sydney
item.description: Emirates flight EK414 from Dubai touched down safely with about 200 Australians on board, after departing Dubai in the early hours of the morning.

item.title: Albanese supported the 'rules-based order' six months ago
item.description: Bipartisan support for the "global rules-based order" has all but disappeared in Australia.

item.title: Tasmanian nurses say lower wages, understaffing a barrier to returning home
item.description: Tasmania needs more nurses, but lower wages and fewer opportunities for career growth remain barriers for enticing health workers to return to their home state.

item.title: Seasonal produce to make the most of in March
item.description: From apples to avocados, these are the early autumn fruit and veggies to look for.

item.title: Real attack or video game? Misinformation and war in the AI age
item.description: Is it real or is it AI? Misinformation seen by millions is spreading online in the wake of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Canada 'unlikely' to join AUKUS despite previous enthusiasm for pact
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and senior Canadian administration figures are in Canberra with defence and security expected to dominate the discussions there.

item.title: Conservative party New Zealand First pushes to make English an official language
item.description: New Zealand might be full of English speakers, but some politicians are pushing for laws to protect the language.

item.title: SA SES uses drone to warn and check in on drivers during flood events
item.description: SA SES says it trialled drone technology for the first time when record rainfall lashed parts of the state over the weekend.

item.title: Australian regulator delays ban on plumbing parts containing lead by two years
item.description: The Australian Building Codes Board delays a ban on plumbing products containing lead by two more years, despite backlash from the industry.

item.title: Hopes for lower melanoma risk as study finds number of moles on children's bodies halved in 25 years
item.description: The study — which surveyed almost 4,000 south-east Queensland children — found average mole count plummeted from 87 in 1992 to 46 in 2016 — a 47 per cent reduction.

item.title: Tasmanian salmon farming emergency licence for antibiotic florfenicol revoked over 'unacceptable risk'
item.description: The use of florfenicol in Tasmania's salmon industry has been banned as the country's veterinary medicine regulator suspends an emergency licence for the antibiotic.

item.title: Australian artists warn government to 'hold its nerve' on AI copyright
item.description: Artists and copyright advocates say big tech is again pushing to loosen Australia's copyright rules as artificial intelligence companies expand.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs Iran
item.description: After winning their opening Women's Asian Cup game, the Matildas now face Iran on the Gold Coast. Here's everything you need to know about how to follow the game.

item.title: Why Iran's most powerful ally is not coming to its aid
item.description: China has condemned the Israel-US war with Iran, but it could benefit Beijing's strategic goals.

item.title: Man charged with manslaughter over death of Jarrad Antonovich in ayahuasca and kambo ceremony
item.description: Jarrad Antonovich died from a perforated oesophagus after allegedly participating in a ceremony involving South American hallucinogen ayahuasca and the frog poison, kambo.

item.title: How Dennis Cometti provided the soundtrack to a generation of AFL football
item.description: The rich, sonorous voice of iconic sports commentator Dennis Cometti, who has died at the age of 76, will be the soundtrack to a generation of kick-to-kick in the parks and backyards of Australia.

item.title: When the 'renovator's delight' is the only house you can afford, here's what to look for
item.description: In the market for a ready-to-renovate property? Here are some tips to help you uncover a gem, from Grand Designs Transformations host Anthony Burke — and a couple who just did it with little DIY experience.

item.title: What to expect in 2026 AFL season as Lions chase third straight premiership
item.description: As the AFL premiership gets underway on Thursday, there will be 17 teams furiously trying to stop the Brisbane Lions winning a third straight flag.

item.title: Laramba residents' court battle with NT government over uranium levels in drinking water hits roadblock
item.description: Residents from the isolated community of Laramba in Central Australia are suing the NT government over claims it failed to address the high levels of uranium in their drinking water.

item.title: Star's former CEO and lawyer broke Corporations Act, Chinese money laundering case judgement finds
item.description: Star casino's former top boss and its legal counsel both breached their director's duties and "failed" to heed warnings about money laundering by Chinese criminal syndicates, the Federal Court in Sydney has found.

item.title: Monash IVF to pay financial settlement to families caught up in embryo bungles
item.description: Fertility giant Monash IVF has agreed to pay financial settlements to families involved in two major bungles that saw two women transferred the wrong embryo.

item.title: Tim Wilson does his best Billy Joel while Angus Taylor gets politicking
item.description: This week in Canberra, Tim Wilson did his best Billy Joel impression, the opposition leader went hard on the government over the cost-of-living crisis and the Canadian prime minister popped into parliament.

item.title: 'It wasn't a great 30 minutes': Blues react to horror quarter in Swans loss
item.description: Carlton is left to reflect on Sydney's dominant third quarter, with the Swans pouring on 75 points as they ran away to complete a convincing victory at the SCG.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Matildas beat Iran, but trail in race for Asian Cup group top spot
item.description: The Matildas beat Iran at Robina to qualify for the knockout stages of the Women's Asian Cup — but four goals are not enough to take them top of their group.

item.title: Astrid Linder designed the world's first average female crash test dummy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Federal government tight-lipped on claims Australians were aboard US sub that sank Iranian warship
item.description: The federal government won't confirm if any Australian personnel were on a US fast-attack submarine that sank an Iranian warship, despite dozens of ADF members rotating through US submarines for training.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Hayley Raso's falconed, Mary Fowler and Amy Sayer sparkle — Matildas vs Iran quick hits
item.description: Hayley Raso cops an unfortunate double hit as Mary Fowler and Amy Sayer sparkle from the start on the Gold Coast. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas vs Iran at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Women are often an 'afterthought' when it comes to designing products
item.description: Phones, cars, and the temperature in an office were all designed to suit the average man. Researchers say women are an afterthought in the way many products and spaces are designed.

item.title: 'Dangerous' Donald Trump chapter means 'nothing is safe' on global financial markets
item.description: A major market meltdown would change the financial plans of millions of Australians overnight. With the Middle East war, how likely is it?

item.title: Nail salon workers voice concern over chemical exposures amid call for research in Australia
item.description: Walk-in nail salons are a mainstay of shopping hubs across Australia, but not enough is known about the health problems nail technicians face. Experts say it is time that changed.

item.title: Polls close in Nepal's first election since deadly uprising forced out government
item.description: Helicopters will be used to collect ballot boxes from snowbound mountain regions across Nepal, home to eight of the world's 10 highest peaks, including Mount Everest.

item.title: Labor MP joins calls for Islamophobia to be added to Bondi royal commission
item.description: Labor backbencher Ed Husic says Islamophobia should be examined as part of Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The Matildas embody Aussie pub rock spirit against Iran
item.description: The Joe Montemurro incarnation of the Matildas feels like a throwback to classic Australian rock. And in their Asian Cup win over Iran, they showed their mantra of playing free, but always going for the win, is starting to pay off.

item.title: Storm Hogan sets a new PB at Branxholme
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Storm Hogan is a barrel racer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Remote residents struggling to afford food amid rising prices, despite grocery subsidy scheme
item.description: The head of Australia's biggest independent remote grocery stores network says in 30 years, he's never seen Indigenous communities struggle so much to afford enough food.

item.title: Mornington Island still in 'crisis' as residents claim taxpayer money being wasted
item.description: A wave of youth suicides lays bare a North Queensland community in crisis, with residents saying years of government investment have failed to create jobs, stability or hope.

item.title: Dementia-friendly choir in Busselton rekindles musical memories
item.description: Regional WA's only dementia-friendly singing group is helping people living with the degenerative condition to reconnect with their musical memories.

item.title: WA government not releasing report on countering violent extremism program
item.description: The WA government will not release an independent report on the effectiveness of its countering violent extremism program, almost two years after police fatally shot one of its radicalised teenage participants.

item.title: Contraceptive pill to be accessible for women in Victoria without prescription from July
item.description: Women getting the contraceptive pill for the first time will be able to access it from Victorian pharmacies without a prescription from July in a move that has "alarmed" GPs.

item.title: Power bills surged when war broke out in 2022. Here's why the Middle East conflict may be different
item.description: The last time a global energy crisis sent Australian power bills skyrocketing was when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. What's changed this time?

item.title: Former senior ACT public servant calls for inquiry into treatment of First Nations staff
item.description: A former head of the Office of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Affairs in the ACT is calling for an inquiry to investigate the treatment and safety of First Nations staff in the territory's public service.

item.title: Evictions of homeless campers by City of Moreton Bay violated human rights, Supreme Court rules
item.description: The Supreme Court has found a south-east Queensland council violated the human rights of homeless campers.

item.title: Anthony Albanese confirms Australian navy personnel on US submarine that sank Iranian vessel
item.description: The prime minister confirms three Royal Australian Navy personnel were on board a US submarine that sank an Iranian warship earlier this week.

item.title: Getting an ADHD diagnosis may become easier, but will it make things better?
item.description: The way ADHD is diagnosed and managed is changing across the country. Here's the reason for the change and how it will work in each state and territory.

item.title: Oil's muted reaction to Trump's attack on Iran
item.description: For all the chaos, the terror and the loss of life in the past week following America's war on Iran, oil prices have managed to maintain a semblance of stability.

item.title: Collingullie Wagga Demons Aussie Rules official steps down over sexist comments
item.description: A southern NSW football club says an official has stepped down after making derogatory comments about players on its women's team.

item.title: West Melbourne mosque reopens to public after police investigate suspicious package
item.description: A mosque in West Melbourne has reopened after the Victoria Police bomb squad was called to investigate a suspicious package.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Plucky Bangladesh teases much-needed jeopardy, North Korea and China are quarterfinal bound
item.description: For 45 minutes, minnows Bangladesh pushed North Korea all the way. It was a breath of fresh air for a tournament desperately lacking close matches.

item.title: Friday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Cats not 'overreacting' after Suns dish out thrashing
item.description: Geelong is outclassed by Gold Coast at Carrara, but Cats coach Chris Scott is remaining calm following his side's 56-point defeat.

item.title: Adelaide man spent his final moments ramped outside hospital, partner says
item.description: An Adelaide man spent some of his final moments stuck in an ambulance outside a major Adelaide public hospital, his partner says.

item.title: Trump redraws the links between Gulf states and rest of the world
item.description: While the cabaret of outlandish and contradictory statements and claims continues in Washington, there is considerable pragmatic pessimism in the Gulf about the mess Donald Trump will leave behind.

item.title: How Japan is addressing overtourism — and what it means for Australians
item.description: Travel operators say overtourism is concentrated in Japan's high-profile destinations including Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka and areas around Mount Fuji.

item.title: Cory Bernardi stands by bestiality claim ahead of SA election
item.description: One Nation's South Australian lead candidate Cory Bernardi says he "100 per cent" stands by highly controversial comments he made almost 14 years ago linking gay marriage to the social acceptance of bestiality.

item.title: Is the US dollar losing reserve currency status? The RBA has some thoughts
item.description: The US dollar's status as a safe-haven currency is questioned at a business gathering in New York.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How an unfancied, injury-hit squad fired the Matildas to their greatest triumph
item.description: Former coach Tom Sermanni says "it wasn't his greatest look". But for its part in helping the Matildas to 2010 Asian Cup glory, it may well be the most consequential makeover in Australian football history.

item.title: Ongoing dry conditions cause ant infestations across South Australia's Riverland
item.description: Prolonged periods of drought have caused swarms of ants to infest homes across South Australia's Riverland

item.title: Decision on sale of Tasmania's largest farm Rushy Lagoon expected soon
item.description: The 22,000-hectare Rushy Lagoon property has long been used for dairy and beef production, but its potential sale to the UK's largest forestry asset manager has raised concerns about agricultural land being used for pine plantations.

item.title: Melissa Lucashenko shares about genesis of new book Not Quite White in the Head
item.description: Melissa Lucashenko is one of Australia's most-acclaimed authors. But when it became clear to the Miles Franklin Literary Award winner that a self-professed fan had only read her non-fiction work, she decided it was time to put together a collection of essays called Not Quite White in the Head.

item.title: 'More pain to come' as oil faces biggest weekly price surge since 1983
item.description: US crude oil has posted its biggest weekly gain in futures trading history, with the war in the Middle East leading to a major disruption to global fuel supplies.

item.title: How Constellations rose from the ashes of the cancelled Adelaide Writers' Week
item.description: Constellations emerged from the ashes of Adelaide Writers' Week, which was cancelled after authors withdrew over censorship. Organisers share how it came together and lessons for the future.

item.title: IDF visualisation of Ayatollah’s bunker in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Anne Aly warns Australia must not 'abandon' people vulnerable to radicalisation
item.description: Radicalisation expert turned federal minister Anne Aly says reaching out to people susceptible to extremism and helping them reconnect to their communities is essential.

item.title: PNG cannot safely store Australian-supplied rifles, military boss warns
item.description: The head of Papua New Guinea's military says the country does not have the capacity to safely store 3,500 rifles Australia plans to ship to the country, raising fears they could end up in the hands of tribal warlords.

item.title: China doesn't want its politicians talking with reporters. Here's what happened when we tried
item.description: China gives foreign journalists one chance each year to speak with politicians. Here’s what happened when I tried.

item.title: Embrace simplicity with baked eggplant and soy-garlic chicken mince - ABC News
item.description: This dish is reminiscent of moussaka but it's a more pared-back meal that's not as rich and involves fewer steps.

item.title: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett rules Rabbitohs out of Zac Lomax pursuit
item.description: Wayne Bennett rules South Sydney out of the Zac Lomax stakes, with the Rabbitohs coach insisting the exiled State of Origin star must live with the consequences of his split with Parramatta.

item.title: Remote NT communities of Daly River, Palumpa evacuated as flood threat continues
item.description: Hundreds of remote residents are being evacuated from their small communities by air this weekend, as flooding inundates parts of the Northern Territory.

item.title: Superstar Sutherland backs up record-breaking ton with key wickets
item.description: An all-round masterclass by Annabel Sutherland has Australia on the brink of victory against India after day two of the day-night Test at the WACA.

item.title: 'Weren't planning on doing it': How Raiders pulled off match-winning play
item.description: Ethan Sanders reveals what went through his mind before he kicked a field goal to take the Raiders to a thrilling 29-28 victory over Manly in golden point.

item.title: Austin Appelbee reunited with rescuers who saved his family in WA's South West
item.description: A WA family who made global headlines when they were rescued after being swept out to sea have been reunited with the local heroes who saved them.

item.title: US posts video of purported strikes on Iran targets on X
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: 'No way' Iran’s footballers can return home, with calls to protect them in Australia
item.description: The Iranian women's football team is facing an impossible situation. Return home, and possibly face punishment from the Islamic Republic regime for its silent protest, or stay in Australia and their families could instead suffer retribution.

item.title: Rapper-turned-politician Balen Shah defeats former prime minister in historic Nepal election
item.description: Balen Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party is set to win a commanding share of parliamentary seats, defeating Nepal’s traditional political heavyweights.

item.title: 'Unexpected' deaths in aged care are rising. One brother demanded to see the coroner
item.description: Attila's shaky phone video of his brother lying dead in aged care documented bruises and blood. It was the start of a long campaign to understand what happened.

item.title: Australia plays an uncomfortable and important role as a middle power
item.description: Middle powers may have a larger role to play in the emerging world order, but the same old dilemma persists: how far are they willing to defend the rules when the countries bending them are their closest allies?

item.title: The Farrer by-election is a test for the Coalition. For voters it's an opportunity
item.description: The upcoming Farrer by-election is already being talked about as a litmus test for the new opposition leader and an ascendant One Nation. It'll also be a measure of major party politics.

item.title: Five works not to miss at Iconic Loved Unexpected at Newcastle Art Gallery
item.description: The newly expanded gallery reopens with a blockbuster exhibition, Iconic Loved Unexpected, that places local artists alongside international stars.

item.title: Iran strikes are Donald Trump's ninth foreign military action in 14 months
item.description: The Australian government has avoided stating whether it supports or opposes many of Trump's operations.

item.title: SA government attributed email to wrong man after his death, traumatising family
item.description: A grieving woman "feels sick" after the government releases an email it claims was written by her deceased partner praising his hospital care, after she said ambulance ramping had denied him dignity in his final hours.

item.title: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran 'very hard' as Tehran clerics vow to pick new leader
item.description: It came as Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian dismissed Mr Trump's demand for the country's surrender as a "dream" and apologised to Gulf states for missile and drone attacks.

item.title: Australia hoping Alyssa Healy bats one last time in Test farewell against India in Perth
item.description: Alyssa Healy is being encouraged to promote herself up the batting order, as the Australia captain prepares for the final act of her international career.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas hope other nations don't have to keep fighting for the basics
item.description: There was a time when the Matildas had to fight and scrap for pay, recognition, and respect. They hope the Women's Asian Cup can change things for other nations.

item.title: Mackay SLSC Surf Boat Rowing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: City of Hobart shipwreck officially found off Gippsland after 150 years
item.description: A renewable energy company's search for an offshore wind farm site leads divers to the wreckage of the City of Hobart, which sank nearly 150 years ago off Victoria's east coast.

item.title: Government considering request for military assistance from Gulf States attacked by Iran
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong says the government is considering requests for military assistance from Gulf countries targeted by Iranian attacks.

item.title: Ian Huntley, one of UK's most notorious child killers, dies after prison attack
item.description: Ian Huntley had been on life support after being hit over the head with a metal bar at Frankland prison in north-east England.

item.title: The K-pop concert that sparked an online war across Asia
item.description: Criticism of Korean fans bringing professional cameras into a Korean boy band concert in Malaysia, against local rules, spiralled into an online war from Vietnam to Indonesia.

item.title: Dolphins and a seal found dead on Eyre Peninsula beaches
item.description: Large, dead marine animals found on Eyre Peninsula beaches spark concern among locals.

item.title: Menopause isn't easy, but for women living in Pilbara heat becomes 'stress multiplier'
item.description: Pilbara woman Bree Maher spent nearly a year trying to find the right support for her menopause symptoms. She said the heat of northern Western Australia amplified her feelings of isolation.

item.title: How to improve the energy efficiency in older Australian homes
item.description: Owners of older homes are being urged to have their energy efficiency assessed, with experts saying improvements will not only make them more comfortable, but help them save money as well.

item.title: Hairdresser-turned-carpenter's career change reflects rise of women in construction trades
item.description: For more than a decade, Haley Miles used scissors every day in her hairdressing business, but, at 32, she picked up tools of a different kind and built a career as a carpenter.

item.title: Are couples happier sleeping separately?
item.description: Despite many people like Hannah finding a "sleep divorce" improves their sleep — and their relationship — a "pervasive stigma" about not sharing a bed persists.

item.title: How some airlines are still flying between Australia, Asia and Europe
item.description: Travel routes between Australia, Asia and Europe are narrowing. Here is how airlines are still flying the Kangaroo Route despite wars in the Middle East and Ukraine.

item.title: Australia and Canada share similar values. Remind us what they are again
item.description: When Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visited Australia last week, he had a message about Australia's and Canada's shared values. He said they were worth defending and could be used to build a more prosperous future.

item.title: Bennett pleads with fans to stay off the field after Johnston's record try
item.description: The Rabbitohs take down the Dolphins in a 70-point clash at Lang Park, with South Sydney's Alex Johnston scoring his 211th career try to move within one of Ken Irvine's record.

item.title: Healy bids farewell as Australia seals crushing Test win over India
item.description: Alyssa Healy signs off her illustrious career in style as Australia wipes away India to claim an easy win in Perth.

item.title: Western Sydney earmarked as potential location for state's second film studio
item.description: The NSW government has earmarked three areas in Western Sydney as possible locations for the state's second film studio.

item.title: Premier's boat trip passed into sanctuary zone while towing lures, RecFish SA concedes
item.description: An election campaign boat trip involving the SA premier has faced criticism after the state's peak recreational fishing body — which says it organised the trip — admitted it breached marine regulations by towing lures in a sanctuary zone.

item.title: Collingwood claims spiteful MCG thriller against St Kilda
item.description: St Kilda's star recruits aren't enough to overcome Collingwood in a thriller at the MCG to cap off Opening Round.

item.title: Murder charges laid over fatal stabbing following argument in Concord house
item.description: Three men were at a home on Spring Street in Concord when emergency services were called following reports of a concern for welfare.

item.title: Matildas finish second in Asian Cup group after pulsating draw with South Korea
item.description: Alanna Kennedy nets a last-gasp equaliser, but it's not enough for the Matildas. They finish second in Group A after a pulsating 3-3 draw with Korea Republic in Sydney.

item.title: When will schools and shops reopen? How Katherine and surrounds will move through flood recovery
item.description: Wondering if schools in the Top End will be open tomorrow? Not sure what the situation is with flood evacuation centres? Looking for the latest information on natural disaster relief payments? We've answered all your NT flood-related questions here.

item.title: F1 Australian Grand Prix quick hits: Oscar Piastri silences the crowd, George Russell predicts Ferrari's 'rocket' launch
item.description: Australia's collective hearts sank as Piastri went into the wall, while race winner George Russell pulled off a prediction that Nostradamus would have been proud of. These are the key moments from qualifying at Albert Park.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Steph Catley floored by head shot and Ellie Carpenter rages at officials — Matildas vs South Korea quick hits
item.description: Steph Catley gets floored and Ellie Carpenter rages as Alanna Kennedy continues her hot streak. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' 3-3 draw against Korea.

item.title: Labor acutely aware of risks in backing US-Israeli attacks on Iran
item.description: The Albanese government will need to be explicit about what it is doing in the Middle East or risk a growing concern that it is being drawn into another war.

item.title: Oil markets can't bank on deal maker Donald Trump in the Middle East
item.description: Investors seem calm, relying on Donald Trump's reputation as a deal maker but the reality is this conflict could be long and destabilising.

item.title: New tourist tax for popular Twelve Apostles on Victoria's Great Ocean Road
item.description: The amount tourists will be charged to visit the Twelve Apostles is subject to consultation with stakeholders.

item.title: Australian crime syndicates using fake tobacco packaging to flood legal market
item.description: Australian crime syndicates are using fake plain packaging — mass produced overseas — to legitimise illicit tobacco and flood the legal market with knock-offs.

item.title: What is a career break and how do you ask for one?
item.description: Whether it’s for a holiday, additional study or a exploring a new field of work, experts say a career break can have many benefits.

item.title: Courtney Moir's Muster Dogs win fuels passion for dog handling, motherhood
item.description: After swapping corporate city life for her family's remote seventh-generation farm, Courtney Moir has claimed the top spot in this year's season of Muster Dogs.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 9: Can you pass the test?
item.description: A royal family photo, Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Australia's first inland city.

item.title: Dubai-based Australian companies caught in crossfire as tensions escalate in Middle East
item.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions mean Australians operating businesses in the Middle East have had to make quick changes to their daily operations.

item.title: Russia a 'big winner' in oil crisis sparked by Middle East war and Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Russia has condemned the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, but its economy and in turn war efforts could be boosted thanks the oil crisis created from the war.

item.title: What will happen to my son when I die?
item.description: Deirdre Croft has dedicated 45 years to caring and advocating for her intellectually disabled son, Richard. Each passing day brings more pressure to secure his future when she's gone. It's a distressing scenario thousands of Australian parents face.

item.title: Israel expands Iran strikes as Tehran moves to name new supreme leader
item.description: Israeli forces expand their bombardment of Iran, striking fuel depots near Tehran as the country moves closer to naming a new supreme leader.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Defiant and proud — Australian Jews speak out in first ABC National Forum
item.description: Along with authors, teachers, students, parents and grandparents, Kahn will tell her story of life in Australia as a Jew in the first ABC National Forum.

item.title: Live: Trump says ending war will be 'mutual' decision with Israeli PM as strikes heard across region
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he has been talking with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who will have input on when to end the war with Iran. Follow live.

item.title: EMDR mental health therapy helped Katie heal, but her court case was discontinued
item.description: Katie Bird sought psychological help for intrusive memories related to alleged sexual abuse. Her court case was discontinued because she underwent eye movement desensitisation and reprocessing (EMDR) therapy.

item.title: Australia sitting on over $1 billion of unused medical research funding as scientists abandon the field
item.description: Researchers say securing funding has become increasingly tough, with many left with no choice but to spend a disproportionate amount of time applying for grants.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Frustrated Matildas exposed by South Korea, but say long road won't derail them
item.description: In the moments that mattered, the Matildas lacked composure, while South Korea made them pay. They'll now have to do it the hard way if they're going to go all the way at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Live: Australian share market sheds more than $100 billion as oil skyrockets
item.description: The All Ordinaries has shed 4pc, or about $120 billion in value, as global markets crumble and oil prices surge15pc towards $US110/barrel. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Two people injured in alleged machete attack in Melbourne CBD
item.description: The alleged attack happened during the busy Formula 1 and Moomba long weekend, and just days after fatal stabbing in the city's north.

item.title: Live: Government won't say which Gulf state requested Australia's military assistance
item.description: The government is currently considering a request to provide military assistance to a Gulf nation, but it won't say which one. Follow live.

item.title: Families leave flooded homes for hotels, caravan parks as much of Queensland remains on flood alert
item.description: Central Highlands residents are being told to move to higher ground, amid car rescues on the Sunshine Coast and in the Gympie region, as much of Queensland deals with the impacts of the tropical low heading for the south-east.

item.title: How the internet reacted to the Matildas' 3-3 draw with South Korea
item.description: The Matildas played out a ding-dong 3-3 draw with Korea Republic in Sydney; the result wasn't enough for Australia to top the group, but the internet was enthralled by the goal-fest.

item.title: Paramedic Harriet Moore honoured for heroic care during fatal ambulance crash
item.description: A NSW South Coast paramedic is recognised for her commitment after continuing to care for patients after sustaining injuries in a fatal crash.

item.title: India dominates New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final to claim back-to-back titles
item.description: India is sent in to bat in the T20 World Cup final and dominates from start to finish to claim its third title.

item.title: Cash-strapped neighbourhood houses seek extra funding amid cost-of-living strain
item.description: More people are turning to Victoria's neighbourhood houses for support as cost-of-living pressures bite. The neighbourhood houses say they can't cope with the extra demand and need state government support.

item.title: Federal funding pulled from WA charity helping people struggling with cost of living, leaving futures uncertain
item.description: As a Perth charity that provides food and financial assistance for people who are struggling loses its federal funding, there are fears it will have to turn more vulnerable people away.

item.title: Ben Tudhope wins snowboard cross silver for Australia at the Winter Paralympics
item.description: Ben Tudhope wins silver in the snowboard cross to register Australia's first medal at the 2026 Winter Paralympics.

item.title: Regional patient 'effectively ramped' for almost three days waiting for care
item.description: When Riverland farmer Mick Evans had a suspected heart attack last year, he was stranded at a regional hospital for almost three days after an airlift request was continually cancelled and there were no available beds at the Royal Adelaide Hospital.

item.title: Protesters surround Iran team bus on Gold Coast after Asian Cup match against the Philippines
item.description: Protesters surround the Iranian women's football team bus after their final match in the Asian Cup, as fears grow for their safety.

item.title: Great Western Highway shut in both directions due to defect at Victoria Pass bridge
item.description: The Great Western Highway between Lithgow and Mount Victoria is closed due to damage discovered on a historical bridge.

item.title: New F1 regulations debut at Australian Grand Prix, with mixed reviews from the paddock
item.description: Formula 1's new era began with a dramatic, chaotic, and compelling grand prix in Melbourne. While views on the regulations are mixed, fans will likely be the ones to decide if it is a success or failure.

item.title: Phosphate Hill fertiliser plant sold to Mayfair, ensuring continued operations at copper smelter
item.description: A major outback mining asset near Mount Isa has been sold to a Queensland-based company for up to $100 million, potentially securing the town's copper production.

item.title: Why Bridget Jones's Diary still resonates with readers 30 years later
item.description: When Oxford-educated Helen Fielding introduced readers to Bridget Jones in her newspaper column, in her own words, she was "quite embarrassed". Three decades later, the book has sold more than 15 million copies worldwide.

item.title: Australia almost defeats Japan at World Baseball Classic as showdown with South Korea looms
item.description: Australia pushes baseball juggernauts Japan all the way in their World Baseball Classic clash.

item.title: Police speaking with 'a number of witnesses' following alleged campsite stabbing near Tamworth
item.description: Police allege an altercation between two men has ended in a fatal stabbing at a campsite near Tamworth.

item.title: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci stars as Port Vale downs Sunderland in FA Cup boilover
item.description: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci and Aussie manager Jon Brady guide Port Vale past Sunderland into the FA Cup quarters for the first time in 72 years.

item.title: Fire at Glasgow Central Station causes evacuations, train disruptions
item.description: A huge and devastating fire near Britain's Glasgow Central Station causes the cancellation of dozens of train services.

item.title: Food supply lines open as Katherine and Daly River under emergency flood warning
item.description: While Katherine remains under an emergency flood warning, water levels are now receding and food supply lines are opening up.

item.title: Who is Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba, a hardline, anti-Western mid-ranking cleric, has been chosen to succeed his late father as Iran's supreme leader.

item.title: How Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth are selling the Iran war to Americans
item.description: The US administration's unapologetic stance on the war has come with a lot of bravado, but there are signs the American public is not convinced.

item.title: Zac Lomax signs two-year Super Rugby deal in major blow to NRL
item.description: Zac Lomax signs a deal with Western Force and Rugby Australia in a major blow to the NRL.

item.title: SA health minister 'very sorry' for leak to media of wrong patient's email
item.description: South Australia's health minister apologises "unreservedly" for the deliberate leak of an email that the government said was from an Adelaide cancer patient who died in 2025, but turned out to be from a different person with the same name.

item.title: Tasmania flags new laws to stop 'good character' evidence as mitigating factor for serious offending
item.description: Tasmania will introduce new laws to stop the use of "good character" references by serious offenders. Advocates say the state has joined a broader national shift to support victim-survivors.

item.title: Macedonian dancers pull out of Moomba parade after flag ban
item.description: The Federation of Macedonian Cultural and Artistic Associations of Victoria says it was a difficult decision but participating without the flag would compromise a key part of their identity.

item.title: Ukraine's war through the letters of its children
item.description: Ukraine's children have grown up amid four years of Vladimir Putin's war. Some who survived have revealed their sadness, hope, torment and aspirations.

item.title: Masked German football fan unplugs VAR monitor in protest during Bundesliga 2 game
item.description: Frustrated football fans take their anger at VAR to the next level, with a masked supporter pulling the plug on a replay monitor during a Bundesliga 2 game.

item.title: Desperation for Iran's women's footballers, as coach says 'we are eager to come back'
item.description: There is an increasing sense of urgency among members of the Iranian Australian community who are desperate to hear from the women's team before they leave Australia, and fear for their safety if they return to Iran.
